stimulus:
While researching a topic, a student has taken the following notes:

  • Ichthyosaurs were marine reptiles that existed millions of years ago.
  • In a 2022 study, Priya Subramanian analyzed fragments of ichthyosaur vertebrae located in the Atacama Desert of Chile.
  • She was initially unsure whether the bones belonged to a single individual or to multiple individuals.
  • She used isotope-ratio analysis to determine that all the fragments had identical chemical signatures.
  • She concluded that the bones belonged to one large adult.
stem: The student wants to present the study and its findings. Which choice most effectively uses relevant information from the notes to accomplish this goal?

answerOptions.content: In a 2022 study, Priya Subramanian determined that ichthyosaur vertebrae located in the Atacama Desert shared identical chemical signatures and thus belonged to a single large adult.
In 2022, Subramanian studied ichthyosaur vertebrae and was initially unsure whether the bones belonged to a single individual or to multiple individuals.
Ichthyosaur vertebrae located in the Atacama Desert were the focus of a 2022 study.
In a 2022 study, Subramanian used isotope-ratio analysis to analyze the vertebrae of ichthyosaurs, marine reptiles that existed millions of years ago.

rationale:
Choice A is the best answer. The sentence presents both the study and its findings, noting the study’s date and the researcher’s name as well as describing what the researcher determined about the vertebrae and how she determined it.

Choice B is incorrect. While the sentence describes the study and the researcher’s initial assessment, it doesn’t present the study’s findings. Choice C is incorrect. While the sentence describes the study and its focus, it doesn’t present the findings or name the researcher. Choice D is incorrect. While the sentence mentions methodology, it doesn’t present the findings.
